'''Mirchidi NeMall''' was a [[Duros/Legends|Duros]], who, shortly before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], bought the [[NeHimmep (freighter)|''NeHimmep'']], a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] which he named after the [[NeHimmep|famed Duros explorer of the same name]]. NeMall never made a good living, but a fear of [[Imperial Office of Customs/Legends|Imperial Customs]] put him off [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]].

==Biography==
Initially a pilot of a [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] spaceline shuttle service, Mirchidi NeMall wanted to own his own ship and work on his own. As such, he saved all he could. Once his service contract was over, he decided to buy a ship.<ref name="Stock Ships" />

NeMall opted for a [[Starfeld Industries/Legends|Starfeld]] [[ZH-25 Questor-class light freighter|ZH-25 Questor]] he would name [[NeHimmep (freighter)|''NeHimmep'']] after [[NeHimmep]], a legendary Duros scout and trader. Although many people in NeMall's situation asked for money to underworld figures, NeMall opted for the [[Duros lending tradition]] and resorted to a Duros lending institution.<ref name="Stock Ships" />

For two years, NeMall managed to get a slight profit while never resorting to illegal jobs. However, c. [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], while he was transporting [[rinzefruit]], the ''NeHimmep'' collided with an uncharted [[asteroid squall]] and all the cargo was lost. NeMall's insurance covered the repairs, but not the cargo expenses.<ref name="Stock Ships" />

Suddenly up to his neck, NeMall changed his policy and began working in well-paid but highly-dangerous runs, some of which might have even been legally questionable. He also began to accept no-question jobs, although he tried his best to avoid smuggling and still managed to get a clean record for the customs — which allowed him to go through frontiers mostly with ease.<ref name="Stock Ships" />

==Personality and traits==
Like many Duros, NeMall was a hard-working, social person who liked scout jobs. He felt more comfortable in space and not under the gravity of a planet.<ref name="Stock Ships" />

A honest being, NeMall always did his best to avoid smuggling. Although he admitted that Imperial Customs were more strict with aliens than with Humans, he was not interested in joining the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. Nonetheless, from 2 ABY onward, NeMall was flexible enough to accept no-question jobs from the Alliance.<ref name="Stock Ships" />

NeMall took the Duros lending tradition very seriously.<ref name="Stock Ships" />

Unlike many freighter owners and captains, NeMall refused to modify his ship beyond what was strictly needed for repairs.<ref name="Stock Ships" />
